stem: A ISP analysis study measures the download speed (Mbps) for a sample of 15 subscribers. The sample mean is 266.5 Mbps with a sample standard deviation of 45.6 Mbps. However, 3 of the 15 observations appear to be outliers (values more than 2.5 standard deviations from the mean). If these outliers are removed, the remaining 12 observations have a mean of 253.9 Mbps and standard deviation of 29.8 Mbps. Estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval that accounts for the uncertainty about whether outliers should be included.

ground_truth.answer: With all data: mean = 266.5, 95% CI = [241.2, 291.8]. Without outliers: mean = 253.9, 95% CI = [236.7, 271.1]. Combined interval accounting for analytic uncertainty: [236.7, 291.8]. Point estimate: 260.2 Mbps.

solution_steps: The outlier treatment choice creates structural uncertainty. Combined interval: [236.7, 291.8].
common_failure_modes: Using only the full sample without considering outlier impact
Removing outliers without reporting both analyses
Not recognizing that the choice of analysis method is itself a source of uncertainty
